United States.title: US Supreme Court overturns Roe v Wade, ending constitutional right to abortion
United States.summary: The US Supreme Court has overturned Roe v Wade, the landmark 1973 decision that established a woman's constitutional right to have an abortion. The ruling in Dobbs v Jackson Women's Health Organization was seen as a major setback for reproductive rights and has sparked protests across the US.

United States.title: US inflation rate hits 40-year high amid rising cost of living
United States.summary: The US inflation rate has hit a 40-year high, with the consumer price index rising 9.1% year-over-year in June. The high inflation rate is expected to put pressure on the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates, which could slow down economic growth.

United States.title: US Supreme Court blocks Biden's student loan forgiveness plan
United States.summary: The US Supreme Court has blocked President Biden's plan to forgive up to $20,000 in student loan debt for millions of Americans. The plan was part of Biden's efforts to address rising student loan debt and income inequality.

South Korea.title: North Korea fires 3 ballistic missiles into Sea of Japan
South Korea.summary: On June 22, three ballistic missiles were launched by North Korea, according to South Korea's Joint Chiefs of Staff. The missiles flew about 250 kilometers (155 miles) before landing in the Sea of Japan. The incident marks the 13th time this year that North Korea has conducted a missile test, with South Korea and the United States criticizing the launches as 'provocative.' The United States has also reaffirmed its commitment to defending South Korea with 'strong and capable' military capabilities.

South Korea.title: South Korea's spy agency accuses North Korea of hacking
South Korea.summary: On June 18, South Korea's National Intelligence Service (NIS) accused North Korea of hacking into the country's IT systems, including a major bank and a university.

South Korea.title: South Korea and China sign economic cooperation agreement
South Korea.summary: On June 15, South Korea and China signed an economic cooperation agreement aimed at strengthening trade ties and promoting investment between the two countries.

Japan.title: Typhoon Mawar brings powerful winds, heavy rain to Japan; at least 2 dead
Japan.summary: Typhoon Mawar makes landfall in Japan, injuring dozens and causing power outages; the storm is expected to move northward, bringing strong winds and heavy rain to the region.

Japan.note: This section is empty because there are no other major security/political/social incidents in Japan for the past 7 days.
